name: Imperial Legion Soldier
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Riders and guards wear full Imperial Legion armor, including boots, cuirass, gauntlets, greaves, helmet, and shield. Armed with steel longswords or claymores. Riders travel on bay horses. Carries lower-class clothing and small amounts of gold.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: vigilant, dutiful, persistent, protective, tireless
Role: Soldiers of the Imperial Legion who serve throughout Cyrodiil in two capacities: riders who patrol back and forth between locations along main roads, and guards who are posted at specific locations, usually inns outside major cities. They never eat or sleep, maintaining constant vigilance. Guards occasionally drink in taverns but remain alert for trouble. They will pursue threats relentlessly—riders have been known to dismount and chase creatures for miles, leaving their horses abandoned for days while tracking down their quarry.
Home Location Information: Found throughout Cyrodiil—riders patrol various routes between settlements, while guards are stationed at specific inns and outposts including Hawkhaven, The Drunken Dragon Inn, Gottshaw Inn, Imperial Bridge Inn, Roxey Inn, Wawnet Inn, and on roads near Oblivion Gates.
Patrol Routes: Riders follow established routes connecting settlements and landmarks: Anvil to Gottshaw Inn, Skingrad to Fort Nikel, Weye to Chorrol, Aleswell to Sardavar Leed, Cheydinhal to Fort Urasek, and Leyawiin to Water's Edge, among others. They ride bay horses at a walk, never faster, maintaining steady presence along Cyrodiil's roads.
Combat Dedication: Imperial Legion Soldiers are relentless in pursuit of threats. Riders will abandon their horses and chase enemies for miles if necessary, demonstrating single-minded commitment to eliminating dangers. They respawn after three days if killed, suggesting either reinforcements or the symbolic representation of the Legion's endless ranks.
Kvatch Reinforcements: During the Battle for Castle Kvatch, three Legion soldiers (two warriors and an archer) arrive at the Chapel of Akatosh after reports of smoke. The leader speaks with those present and all three agree to follow and assist in the battle, bringing professional military support to the desperate defense.
Personality - The Unwavering Patrol: Imperial Legion Soldiers embody tireless dedication to duty. They patrol endlessly without rest, maintaining the Empire's presence along every major road and at key locations throughout Cyrodiil. Their refusal to sleep or eat suggests either supernatural endurance or symbolic representation of the Legion's constant vigilance—the sun may set, but the Legion never rests. Their dogged pursuit of threats, even at the cost of abandoning their horses and chasing creatures for days, demonstrates commitment that borders on obsession. They are the visible face of Imperial authority, a reassuring presence for travelers and a warning to bandits and beasts. When danger arises—whether a creature attack, an Oblivion Gate, or a besieged city—they respond without hesitation, ready to lay down their lives in service to the Empire and its people.
author: Bethesda
